items.title: Defence industry could rival WA resources sector under weapons hub plan

children.content: Missiles for use in the Australian military could soon be made in Western Australia with the government seeking to establish a local weapons manufacturing hub.

gallery.altText: A mid-shot of WA Premier Roger Cook giving a speech at an orange dais on stage wearing a suit and tie, against a black backdrop.
Roger Cook and Paul Papalia in discussion as they walk among a crowd at a defence conference, with a blonde woman on the right.

thumbnail.altText: A mid-shot of WA Premier Roger Cook giving a speech at an orange dais on stage wearing a suit and tie, against a black backdrop.
Roger Cook and Paul Papalia in discussion as they walk among a crowd at a defence conference, with a blonde woman on the right.

items.title: After losing her 3yo daughter, Perth mum Jess says MDMA therapy saved her life

image.altText: Woman with short brown hair looks at camera in a green silk shirt and in a well lit therapy room.

children.content: Jess Yugovich is among the Australians trying MDMA-assisted psychotherapy to treat PTSD, as highly regulated facilities process the drug for therapeutic use at clinics like one in Perth.

items.title: 'Inhumane' disability home allegedly had fridges locked as residents slept on floor

image.altText: a house and a leafy front yard

children.content: Former workers at a disability home in Western Australia's far north raise allegations of neglect, including medical mismanagement, locked fridges and a child being placed at risk.

items.title: Leaked documents about WA mines reveal how far BHP really is from net zero goals

children.content: BHP's WA iron ore mines are only forecast to reduce emissions by about 1 per cent by 2030, and the mining giant has also delayed renewable energy projects in the Pilbara — raising doubts about its ambitious climate goal.

articles.title: Mining giant BHP's plan to go green was 'urgent', until it wasn't

children.content: Hundreds of pages of internal BHP documents reveal how the company sold itself as a climate leader while internally finding reasons to put off action in the Pilbara.

image.altText: A collage graphic showing a mining truck, wind turbines, solar panels a train and excerpts from documents.

gallery.altText: A large yellow mining truck drives along a raise road. It has a load in its tray.
An aerial shot of the front of a BHP freight train on one set of parallel train tracks running through red dirt and scrub.
Aerial photo of an enormous open-cut mine site stretching across the frame and towards the horizon.

thumbnail.altText: A large yellow mining truck drives along a raise road. It has a load in its tray.
An aerial shot of the front of a BHP freight train on one set of parallel train tracks running through red dirt and scrub.
Aerial photo of an enormous open-cut mine site stretching across the frame and towards the horizon.

items.title: Farmers hopeful cold front will help WA's dry May problem

children.content: A downward trend in autumn rainfall has WA farmers adapting to early seeding, but hoping this week's forecast cold front will provide a much-needed break to the dry spell.

image.altText: A tractor pulling crop planting equipment in a paddock sits underneath a vibrant and stunning sunset

items.title: Should bore water be sold as spring water?

children.content: A resident of the Perth Hills, where thousands of litres of groundwater are extracted each week and sold as bottled water, has questioned whether it's accurate to label it as 'spring water'.

image.altText: Bottled water stacked on fridge shelves.

items.title: Why it's about to get so much harder to find an aged care bed in WA

children.content: It's going to get much harder to find an available aged care bed in WA, a new report finds, with a "substantial and time-critical shortfall" predicted.

gallery.altText: A close-up photo of an elderly woman knitting.
Simone stands with a neutral expression in an office hallway.
A bed in a resident's room in an aged care home.

thumbnail.altText: A close-up photo of an elderly woman knitting.
Simone stands with a neutral expression in an office hallway.
A bed in a resident's room in an aged care home.

items.title: Activists protest about treatment of Gaza flotilla at Parliament House demonstration

children.content: Activists stage a protest at Parliament House in Canberra against Israel's treatment of Gaza flotilla activists. Follow live.

image.altText: Officers usher away a protester in parliament house.

items.title: 'ISIS bride' under exclusion order not returning to Australia, ABC confirms

children.content: The majority of the final group of Australians living in a notorious Syrian camp for the families of killed and captured Islamic State militants are expected to arrive in Australia soon. Here's what we know about them.

image.altText: A group of three women wearing burqas walk along a camp in Syria.

items.title: Brad Scott sacked by Essendon ahead of Eagles clash after one win in past 24 matches

children.content: Essendon sacks head coach Brad Scott ahead of Sunday's game against the West Coast Eagles in Perth as the Bombers languish at the bottom of the AFL ladder, with Dean Solomon set to take over as interim coach.

gallery.altText: Essendon Bombers head coach Brad Scott walks off the ground, looking downward

thumbnail.altText: Essendon Bombers head coach Brad Scott walks off the ground, looking downward

items.title: Labor to ignore call for pause on CGT changes

children.content: The government is pushing ahead with capital gains tax changes while consulting on limited carve-outs.

image.altText: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in a grey suit, staring to his right, with the Australian flag behind him.

items.title: How to check if your vaccines are up to date as diphtheria outbreak continues

children.content: Do you know your vaccination history? Some vaccines need to be updated regularly, especially if you are travelling. Here's what you need to know to ensure you stay protected.

image.altText: A health worker puts a bandaid over a spot where someone was vaccinated.

items.title: Notorious Perth paedophile Bradley Pen Dragon released from jail again

children.content: A notorious Perth paedophile who breached his prison release conditions 11 times has again been freed into the community, as the child protection minister argues it may be unconstitutional to keep him in custody.

items.title: Man, 23, charged with murder over death of eight-week-old baby

children.content: A 23-year-old man has been charged with murder over the death of an eight-week-old child in suburban Perth.

image.altText: A door of a police car with police tape tied to the side mirror.

items.title: Australia's first female astronaut closer than ever to maiden space mission

children.content: The European Space Agency has formally extended the offer for Australia's Katherine Bennell-Pegg to join a mission that would take her to the International Space Station, but the place is not guaranteed.

gallery.altText: An astronaut with a globe of the earth.
An astronaut with high school students in Kalgoorlie.

thumbnail.altText: An astronaut with a globe of the earth.
An astronaut with high school students in Kalgoorlie.

items.title: Government sets up fight with Santos over gas reservation plan

children.content: The draft framework outlines a requirement for gas producers to supply 20 per cent of their export to the domestic market, which the gas lobby argues will "undermine" the industry.

image.altText: A woman in a blue jacket looking pensive.

items.title: Bringing Them Home inaction a 'woeful failure', ex-commissioner says

children.content: Mick Dodson heard "soul-destroying" testimony from more than 500 First Nations people during a landmark inquiry. He says that 29 years later, it's time for action.

image.altText: A man, wearing a tshirt and an akubra hat, stands outside in front of trees.

items.title: Alleged hit-and-run driver gave himself up after freeway death, police say

children.content: A 22-year-old man dies after being struck by a car on the Mitchell Freeway in Perth's north, with the alleged driver facing court charged over the incident after he later went to a police station.

gallery.altText: A wide shot of an empty strethc of the Mitchell Freeway after a fatla crash, with several vehicles on either side of the road.
A yellow 'Road closed' sign sits on the road in front of traffic at an intersection.

thumbnail.altText: A wide shot of an empty strethc of the Mitchell Freeway after a fatla crash, with several vehicles on either side of the road.
A yellow 'Road closed' sign sits on the road in front of traffic at an intersection.

items.title: Using AI to manage your budget? This is what experts want you to know

children.content: "Hallucinations", privacy and other factors to consider if you're turning to artificial intelligence for financial advice.

image.altText: The hands hands of a woman typing, while she's reclined on the sofa.

items.title: What do plummeting clearance rates mean for the property market?

children.content: Early signs of an Australian housing market correction are now clearly visible, and at the heart of the recent property market weakness is less vendor power.

image.altText: An overhead shot of a housing estate.

items.title: 'My time to go': The words that showed Daniher's humanity in tragedy

children.content: Neale Daniher showed leadership on and off the field throughout his life, from club captaincy to coaching and as Australian of the Year — but no more so when facing the challenge of an MND diagnosis.

items.title: NACC chief Brereton to face Senate grilling after shock resignation

children.content: National Anti-Corruption Commissioner Paul Brereton will face a grilling by senators at estimates hearings today following his shock resignation on Monday, amid calls for a wider reset of the troubled agency.

image.altText: Paul Brereton speaks during a committee hearing.

items.title: Perth's Cottesloe revamp to go ahead but warning more funding needed

children.content: An oceanfront redevelopment at Perth's iconic Cottesloe Beach will go ahead with $15 million in funding, but the mayor says more financial assistance is needed from the state government to complete the project.

gallery.altText: Two women with one wearing hijab siting up and a couple next to them lying on the lawn by beach.
The design rendering of the Cottesloe Beach foreshore project
A car park sign by Cottesloe Beach saying "3 hour parking limit at all times".
